metadata.description: Symptom Knowledge Graph for Rural Health Pre-Screening System - India
metadata.guidelines_reference: WHO Emergency Triage Assessment and Treatment (ETAT)

red_flag_combinations.reason: Possible acute coronary syndrome or pulmonary embolism
Classic presentation of myocardial infarction (heart attack)
Strong indicators of acute stroke — FAST protocol activation required
Possible meningitis — requires immediate lumbar puncture and antibiotics
Status epilepticus or severe neurological emergency
Severe respiratory failure — immediate oxygen and intubation may be required
Possible ectopic pregnancy or obstetric emergency
Possible GI hemorrhage or bowel obstruction
Possible raised intracranial pressure or hypertensive emergency
Possible dengue, chikungunya, or other arboviral infection
Severe gastroenteritis with risk of hypovolemic shock — common in rural India
Possible severe pneumonia or acute respiratory infection
Classic triad for pulmonary tuberculosis — immediate sputum test required
Possible acute hepatitis, cholangitis, or liver abscess
Possible retinal artery occlusion, optic neuritis, or stroke
Febrile seizure or CNS infection — especially dangerous in children
Possible pre-eclampsia or eclampsia — life-threatening obstetric emergency
Possible upper GI bleed with hemodynamic instability
Possible inhalational burn injury — airway compromise risk
Possible bacterial meningitis or encephalitis
